subject: Geology
title: Deep Time
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Deep Time.

hook.text: Earth is 4.5 billion years old. If its history were a single day, humans would appear in the last second.

buildup.text: Deep time is the concept that Earth's history spans incomprehensibly vast stretches of time.

discovery.text: Mountains rise and erode, oceans open and close, and species appear and vanish, all over millions of years.

twist.text: Humans struggle to grasp deep time. We think in decades. Geology thinks in millions and billions of years.

climax.text: Deep time changes how you see the world. Nothing is permanent. Even continents are just passing through.

punchline.text: In deep time, mountains are temporary.
